- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  js:
    # Keep Playwright screenshot tests off `ubuntu-latest`, whose image churn
    # can shift flex layout/heights against committed Linux baselines.
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    timeout-minutes: 25
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # packageManager field in repo root package.json selects the pnpm version (do not duplicate here).

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: pnpm

      - run: pnpm install

      - run: pnpm verify

      - run: cd packages/web && pnpm exec playwright install chromium --with-deps

      - run: cd packages/web && pnpm exec playwright test
        env:
          CI: true

      - name: Evidence CI correlation hint (bundles reviewer mapping)
        if: always()
        env:
          CORR_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          CORR_SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
          CORR_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
          CORR_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
        run: |
          mkdir -p packages/web/test-results
          {
            echo "Map Playwright PNGs to semantic evidence fields:"
            echo "  GET /api/models/{id}/evidence-package -> suggestedEvidenceArtifactBasename,"
            echo "  semanticDigestPrefix16, suggestedEvidenceBundleFilenames.evidencePackageJson,"
            echo "  deterministicSheetEvidence[].correlation + playwrightSuggestedFilenames (png/svg/pdf/rasterPlaceholderProbe),"
            echo "  deterministicSheetEvidence[].printRasterPngHref + sheetPrintRasterIngest_v1"
            echo "    (svgContentSha256, placeholderPngSha256, diffCorrelation.playwrightBaselineSlot e.g. pngFullSheet),"
            echo "  GET /api/models/{id}/exports/sheet-print-raster.png — deterministic 128×112 print-surrogate PNG (96px viewport layout stamp + 16px titleblock band + SVG salt);"
            echo "    response headers: X-Bim-Ai-Sheet-Print-Raster-Contract, X-Bim-Ai-Sheet-Svg-Sha256, width/height."
            echo "  deterministicPlanViewEvidence[].playwrightSuggestedFilenames.pngPlanCanvas,"
            echo "  deterministicSectionCutEvidence[].playwrightSuggestedFilenames.pngSectionViewport,"
            echo "  deterministic3dViewEvidence[].playwrightSuggestedFilenames.pngViewport."
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.expectedDeterministicPngBasenames — flat CI grep target vs PNG artifact names;"
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.pixelDiffExpectation — reserved metadata when attaching programmatic diff outputs;"
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.screenshotHintGaps_v1 — deterministic rows missing required Playwright PNG filename slots;"
            echo "  evidenceLifecycleSignal_v1 — programmatic rollup (counts + digest + basename) for scripts/agents;"
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.pixelDiffExpectation.artifactIngestCorrelation_v1.ingestManifestDigestSha256 — deterministic SHA-256 over canonical ingest checklist pairs (sorted baseline/diff basenames)."
            echo "  evidenceLifecycleSignal_v1.artifactIngestManifestDigestSha256 — mirrors ingest correlation digest when pixelDiffExpectation carries artifactIngestCorrelation_v1."
            echo "  evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1.blockerCodes — grep: artifact_ingest_correlation_digest_mismatch (ingest correlation digest must match ingestChecklist_v1.targets recompute)."
            echo "  evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1 — derivative blocker rollup (correlation, screenshot slots, pixel diff ingest, artifact_ingest_correlation_digest_mismatch) + agent remediate action hook."
            echo "  evidenceReviewPerformanceGate_v1 — advisory mock gate (gateClosed, blockerCodesEcho, advisoryBudgetHintsMs_v1); derived from evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1 only; no wall-clock probes."
            echo "  evidenceBaselineLifecycleReadout_v1 — per-target baseline lifecycle rollup (committedFixtureStatus, digestCorrelationStatus, suggestedNextAction, ciGateHint); digest-excluded derivative summary."
            echo "  semanticDigestSha256 omits evidenceReviewPerformanceGate_v1 (derivative rollup; stable row digests)."
            echo "  evidenceAgentFollowThrough_v1 — stagedArtifactLinks_v1 (resolutionMode, exportRelativePaths, env-gated GitHub Actions run URLs;"
            echo "    no secrets/network), plus BCF/issue coordination checks, evidenceRef gaps, replayConflict hints (digest-excluded)."
            echo "  Python pytest scale gate: test_bundle_replay_diagnostics_large_list_under_budget (replayDiagnostics metadata scan)."
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.pixelDiffExpectation.thresholdPolicy_v1 — advisory diff thresholds for client ingest."
            echo ""
            echo "This CI run:"
            echo "  github.run_id=${CORR_RUN_ID}"
            echo "  github.sha=${CORR_SHA}"
            echo "  workflow=${CORR_WORKFLOW} job=${CORR_JOB}"
            echo "  playwright artifact name=evidence-web-${CORR_RUN_ID}-playwright"
            echo ""
            echo "Recipe: download that artifact -> open packages/web/playwright-report/index.html"
            echo "  -> match failing or baseline PNG names to evidence-package deterministic* rows"
            echo "  and agentEvidenceClosureHints.ciArtifactRelativePaths."
          } > packages/web/test-results/ci-evidence-correlation-hint.txt

      - name: Upload Playwright report + screenshots
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: evidence-web-${{ github.run_id }}-playwright
          retention-days: 14
          path: |
            packages/web/playwright-report
            packages/web/test-results
            packages/web/e2e/__screenshots__/evidence-baselines/evidence-baselines.spec.ts

  python:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: app
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.12'

      - name: Install uv
        run: pip install uv

      - name: Verify lockfile is up to date
        run: uv lock --check

      - name: Install Python deps from lockfile
        run: uv sync --frozen --extra dev --extra ifc

      - name: Import FastAPI app (route wiring regression guard)
        run: PYTHONPATH=. uv run python -c "from bim_ai.main import app"

      - run: ruff check bim_ai tests

      - run: pytest

      - name: Optional evidence-package staging probe
        env:
          BIM_AI_EVIDENCE_PROBE_BASE_URL: ${{ vars.BIM_AI_EVIDENCE_PROBE_BASE_URL }}
          BIM_AI_EVIDENCE_PROBE_MODEL_ID: ${{ vars.BIM_AI_EVIDENCE_PROBE_MODEL_ID }}
        run: bash scripts/ci-evidence-package-probe.sh

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  js:
    # Keep Playwright screenshot tests off `ubuntu-latest`, whose image churn
    # can shift flex layout/heights against committed Linux baselines.
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    timeout-minutes: 25
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # packageManager field in repo root package.json selects the pnpm version (do not duplicate here).

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: pnpm

      - run: pnpm install

      - run: pnpm verify

      - run: cd packages/web && pnpm exec playwright install chromium --with-deps

      - run: cd packages/web && pnpm exec playwright test
        env:
          CI: true

      - name: Evidence CI correlation hint (bundles reviewer mapping)
        if: always()
        env:
          CORR_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          CORR_SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
          CORR_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
          CORR_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
        run: |
          mkdir -p packages/web/test-results
          {
            echo "Map Playwright PNGs to semantic evidence fields:"
            echo "  GET /api/models/{id}/evidence-package -> suggestedEvidenceArtifactBasename,"
            echo "  semanticDigestPrefix16, suggestedEvidenceBundleFilenames.evidencePackageJson,"
            echo "  deterministicSheetEvidence[].correlation + playwrightSuggestedFilenames (png/svg/pdf/rasterPlaceholderProbe),"
            echo "  deterministicSheetEvidence[].printRasterPngHref + sheetPrintRasterIngest_v1"
            echo "    (svgContentSha256, placeholderPngSha256, diffCorrelation.playwrightBaselineSlot e.g. pngFullSheet),"
            echo "  GET /api/models/{id}/exports/sheet-print-raster.png — deterministic 128×112 print-surrogate PNG (96px viewport layout stamp + 16px titleblock band + SVG salt);"
            echo "    response headers: X-Bim-Ai-Sheet-Print-Raster-Contract, X-Bim-Ai-Sheet-Svg-Sha256, width/height."
            echo "  deterministicPlanViewEvidence[].playwrightSuggestedFilenames.pngPlanCanvas,"
            echo "  deterministicSectionCutEvidence[].playwrightSuggestedFilenames.pngSectionViewport,"
            echo "  deterministic3dViewEvidence[].playwrightSuggestedFilenames.pngViewport."
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.expectedDeterministicPngBasenames — flat CI grep target vs PNG artifact names;"
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.pixelDiffExpectation — reserved metadata when attaching programmatic diff outputs;"
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.screenshotHintGaps_v1 — deterministic rows missing required Playwright PNG filename slots;"
            echo "  evidenceLifecycleSignal_v1 — programmatic rollup (counts + digest + basename) for scripts/agents;"
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.pixelDiffExpectation.artifactIngestCorrelation_v1.ingestManifestDigestSha256 — deterministic SHA-256 over canonical ingest checklist pairs (sorted baseline/diff basenames)."
            echo "  evidenceLifecycleSignal_v1.artifactIngestManifestDigestSha256 — mirrors ingest correlation digest when pixelDiffExpectation carries artifactIngestCorrelation_v1."
            echo "  evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1.blockerCodes — grep: artifact_ingest_correlation_digest_mismatch (ingest correlation digest must match ingestChecklist_v1.targets recompute)."
            echo "  evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1 — derivative blocker rollup (correlation, screenshot slots, pixel diff ingest, artifact_ingest_correlation_digest_mismatch) + agent remediate action hook."
            echo "  evidenceReviewPerformanceGate_v1 — advisory mock gate (gateClosed, blockerCodesEcho, advisoryBudgetHintsMs_v1); derived from evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1 only; no wall-clock probes."
            echo "  evidenceBaselineLifecycleReadout_v1 — per-target baseline lifecycle rollup (committedFixtureStatus, digestCorrelationStatus, suggestedNextAction, ciGateHint); digest-excluded derivative summary."
            echo "  semanticDigestSha256 omits evidenceReviewPerformanceGate_v1 (derivative rollup; stable row digests)."
            echo "  evidenceAgentFollowThrough_v1 — stagedArtifactLinks_v1 (resolutionMode, exportRelativePaths, env-gated GitHub Actions run URLs;"
            echo "    no secrets/network), plus BCF/issue coordination checks, evidenceRef gaps, replayConflict hints (digest-excluded)."
            echo "  Python pytest scale gate: test_bundle_replay_diagnostics_large_list_under_budget (replayDiagnostics metadata scan)."
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.pixelDiffExpectation.thresholdPolicy_v1 — advisory diff thresholds for client ingest."
            echo ""
            echo "This CI run:"
            echo "  github.run_id=${CORR_RUN_ID}"
            echo "  github.sha=${CORR_SHA}"
            echo "  workflow=${CORR_WORKFLOW} job=${CORR_JOB}"
            echo "  playwright artifact name=evidence-web-${CORR_RUN_ID}-playwright"
            echo ""
            echo "Recipe: download that artifact -> open packages/web/playwright-report/index.html"
            echo "  -> match failing or baseline PNG names to evidence-package deterministic* rows"
            echo "  and agentEvidenceClosureHints.ciArtifactRelativePaths."
          } > packages/web/test-results/ci-evidence-correlation-hint.txt

      - name: Upload Playwright report + screenshots
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: evidence-web-${{ github.run_id }}-playwright
          retention-days: 14
          path: |
            packages/web/playwright-report
            packages/web/test-results
            packages/web/e2e/__screenshots__/evidence-baselines/evidence-baselines.spec.ts

  python:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: app
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.12'

      - name: Install uv
        run: pip install uv

      - name: Verify lockfile is up to date
        run: uv lock --check

      - name: Install Python deps from lockfile
        run: uv sync --frozen --extra dev --extra ifc

      - name: Import FastAPI app (route wiring regression guard)
        run: PYTHONPATH=. uv run python -c "from bim_ai.main import app"

      - run: ruff check bim_ai tests

      - run: pytest

      - name: Optional evidence-package staging probe
        env:
          BIM_AI_EVIDENCE_PROBE_BASE_URL: ${{ vars.BIM_AI_EVIDENCE_PROBE_BASE_URL }}
          BIM_AI_EVIDENCE_PROBE_MODEL_ID: ${{ vars.BIM_AI_EVIDENCE_PROBE_MODEL_ID }}
        run: bash scripts/ci-evidence-package-probe.sh

class SetToolPrefCmd(BaseModel):
    """CHR-V3-08: Store a sticky tool-modifier preference for the session.

    ``tool`` is the authoring tool name (e.g. "wall", "door", "window").
    ``pref_key`` is the modifier name (e.g. "alignment", "swingSide", "multipleMode").
    ``pref_value`` is the serialised value (always a string; booleans as "true"/"false").
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["setToolPref"] = "setToolPref"
    tool: str
    pref_key: str = Field(alias="prefKey")
    pref_value: str = Field(alias="prefValue")

class SetToolPrefCmd(BaseModel):
    """CHR-V3-08: Store a sticky tool-modifier preference for the session.

    ``tool`` is the authoring tool name (e.g. "wall", "door", "window").
    ``pref_key`` is the modifier name (e.g. "alignment", "swingSide", "multipleMode").
    ``pref_value`` is the serialised value (always a string; booleans as "true"/"false").
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["setToolPref"] = "setToolPref"
    tool: str
    pref_key: str = Field(alias="prefKey")
    pref_value: str = Field(alias="prefValue")

"""IMG-V3-01 — Deterministic CV trace pipeline.

Entry point: trace(image_path, ...) -> StructuredLayout

Pipeline:
  1. Load image → get pixel dimensions
  2. SKB-14 edge detection (deterministic Canny)
  3. SKB-04 calibrator → derive mm_per_px from edge image
  4. SKB-07 colour sampler → populate detectedTypeKey per room
  5. polygon.recover_rooms() → rooms, walls, openings
  6. ocr.extract_labels() → OCR labels (graceful fallback)
  7. Assemble StructuredLayout

No AI runs inside this module. Same input → byte-identical JSON output.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

from bim_ai.img.types import Advisory, ImageMetadata, RoomRegion, StructuredLayout
from bim_ai.skb.edge_detection import detect_edges

_FALLBACK_SCALE_MM_PER_PX = 1.0
_LOW_CONTRAST_DENSITY_THRESHOLD = 0.001


def trace(
    image_path: str | Path,
    *,
    archetype_hint: str | None = None,
    brief_path: str | None = None,
) -> StructuredLayout:
    """Deterministic CV trace: image → StructuredLayout.

    ``archetype_hint`` and ``brief_path`` are passed through as metadata only
    — the CV pipeline does not interpret them.

    Raises FileNotFoundError if the image does not exist.
    """
    from bim_ai.img.ocr import extract_labels
    from bim_ai.img.polygon import recover_rooms

    in_path = Path(image_path)
    if not in_path.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Image not found: {in_path}")

    # 1. Load image dimensions (lazy import for speed).
    cv_img = None
    try:
        from PIL import Image as _Image

        with _Image.open(str(in_path)) as img:
            width_px, height_px = img.size
    except Exception:
        try:
            import cv2  # type: ignore[import-not-found]

            cv_img = cv2.imread(str(in_path))
            if cv_img is None:
                raise ValueError(f"Cannot read image: {in_path}")
            height_px, width_px = cv_img.shape[:2]
        except Exception as exc:
            raise ValueError(f"Cannot load image dimensions: {exc}") from exc

    # 2. SKB-14 edge detection — write to a deterministic temp path keyed by
    # the input file's content hash so repeated calls reuse the same output.
    img_hash = _file_sha256_prefix(in_path)
    tmp_dir = Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / "bim_ai_img"
    tmp_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    edges_path = tmp_dir / f"edges_{img_hash}.png"

    edge_result = detect_edges(
        str(in_path),
        str(edges_path),
        canny_low=50,
        canny_high=150,
        blur_sigma=1.4,
    )

    advisories: list[Advisory] = []

    # 3. SKB-04 calibrator — derive mm_per_px from edge image.
    # Falls back to 1.0 if the edge image cannot be read or yields no contours.
    scale = _FALLBACK_SCALE_MM_PER_PX
    try:
        from bim_ai.skb.calibrator import calibrate_from_edges

        edges_arr = _load_edge_array(str(edges_path))
        if edges_arr is not None:
            derived = calibrate_from_edges(edges_arr)
            if derived > 0:
                scale = derived
    except Exception:
        scale = _FALLBACK_SCALE_MM_PER_PX

    # 4. Polygon recovery (Hough + contours)
    rooms, walls, openings, poly_advisories = recover_rooms(
        edges_path=str(edges_path),
        scale_mm_per_px=scale,
        image_width_px=width_px,
        image_height_px=height_px,
    )
    advisories.extend(poly_advisories)

    # 5. SKB-07 colour sampler — populate detectedTypeKey per room.
    rooms = _apply_colour_type_hints(in_path, rooms, cv_img)

    # 6. OCR (graceful fallback)
    ocr_labels, ocr_advisories = extract_labels(str(in_path), scale)
    advisories.extend(ocr_advisories)

    # Check for no_dimensions_detected advisory (OCR found no numeric labels).
    has_numeric = any(any(c.isdigit() for c in lbl.text) for lbl in ocr_labels)
    if not has_numeric and not any(a.code == "tesseract_unavailable" for a in advisories):
        advisories.append(Advisory(code="no_dimensions_detected"))

    # 7. Assemble
    layout = StructuredLayout(
        schemaVersion="img-v3.0",
        imageMetadata=ImageMetadata(
            widthPx=width_px,
            heightPx=height_px,
            calibrationMmPerPx=scale,
            briefPath=str(brief_path) if brief_path else None,
        ),
        rooms=rooms,
        walls=walls,
        openings=openings,
        ocrLabels=ocr_labels,
        advisories=_dedup_advisories(advisories),
    )
    return layout


def _load_edge_array(edges_path: str):  # type: ignore[return]
    """Load an edge image as a numpy uint8 greyscale array. Returns None on failure."""
    try:
        import cv2  # type: ignore[import-not-found]

        arr = cv2.imread(edges_path, cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)
        return arr  # may be None if file missing
    except ImportError:
        pass
    try:
        import numpy as np  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
        from PIL import Image as _Image

        arr = np.asarray(_Image.open(edges_path).convert("L"), dtype=np.uint8)
        return arr
    except Exception:
        return None


def _apply_colour_type_hints(
    image_path: Path,
    rooms: list[RoomRegion],
    cv_img: Any | None,  # type: ignore[name-defined]
) -> list[RoomRegion]:
    """Call SKB-07 colour sampler on each room polygon to populate detectedTypeKey.

    Returns a new list with updated rooms (immutable Pydantic model_copy).
    Gracefully no-ops if colour_sampler is unavailable or image cannot be loaded.
    """
    try:
        from bim_ai.skb.colour_sampler import sample
    except ImportError:
        return rooms

    # Load BGR image array once.
    img_arr = cv_img
    if img_arr is None:
        try:
            import cv2  # type: ignore[import-not-found]

            img_arr = cv2.imread(str(image_path))
        except ImportError:
            try:
                import numpy as np  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
                from PIL import Image as _Image

                pil = _Image.open(str(image_path)).convert("RGB")
                arr = np.asarray(pil, dtype=np.uint8)
                img_arr = arr[..., ::-1].copy()  # RGB → BGR
            except Exception:
                img_arr = None

    if img_arr is None:
        return rooms

    updated: list[RoomRegion] = []
    for room in rooms:
        type_key = room.detected_type_key
        if type_key is None:
            try:
                pixel_pts = [(int(pt.x), int(pt.y)) for pt in room.polygon_mm]
                if len(pixel_pts) >= 3:
                    type_key = sample(img_arr, pixel_pts)
            except Exception:
                type_key = None
        if type_key is not None and type_key != room.detected_type_key:
            room = room.model_copy(update={"detected_type_key": type_key})
        updated.append(room)
    return updated


def _file_sha256_prefix(path: Path, n: int = 16) -> str:
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with open(path, "rb") as f:

"""IMG-V3-01 — Deterministic CV trace pipeline.

Entry point: trace(image_path, ...) -> StructuredLayout

Pipeline:
  1. Load image → get pixel dimensions
  2. SKB-14 edge detection (deterministic Canny)
  3. SKB-04 calibrator → derive mm_per_px from edge image
  4. SKB-07 colour sampler → populate detectedTypeKey per room
  5. polygon.recover_rooms() → rooms, walls, openings
  6. ocr.extract_labels() → OCR labels (graceful fallback)
  7. Assemble StructuredLayout

No AI runs inside this module. Same input → byte-identical JSON output.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

from bim_ai.img.types import Advisory, ImageMetadata, RoomRegion, StructuredLayout
from bim_ai.skb.edge_detection import detect_edges

_FALLBACK_SCALE_MM_PER_PX = 1.0
_LOW_CONTRAST_DENSITY_THRESHOLD = 0.001


def trace(
    image_path: str | Path,
    *,
    archetype_hint: str | None = None,
    brief_path: str | None = None,
) -> StructuredLayout:
    """Deterministic CV trace: image → StructuredLayout.

    ``archetype_hint`` and ``brief_path`` are passed through as metadata only
    — the CV pipeline does not interpret them.

    Raises FileNotFoundError if the image does not exist.
    """
    from bim_ai.img.ocr import extract_labels
    from bim_ai.img.polygon import recover_rooms

    in_path = Path(image_path)
    if not in_path.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Image not found: {in_path}")

    # 1. Load image dimensions (lazy import for speed).
    cv_img = None
    try:
        from PIL import Image as _Image

        with _Image.open(str(in_path)) as img:
            width_px, height_px = img.size
    except Exception:
        try:
            import cv2  # type: ignore[import-not-found]

            cv_img = cv2.imread(str(in_path))
            if cv_img is None:
                raise ValueError(f"Cannot read image: {in_path}")
            height_px, width_px = cv_img.shape[:2]
        except Exception as exc:
            raise ValueError(f"Cannot load image dimensions: {exc}") from exc

    # 2. SKB-14 edge detection — write to a deterministic temp path keyed by
    # the input file's content hash so repeated calls reuse the same output.
    img_hash = _file_sha256_prefix(in_path)
    tmp_dir = Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / "bim_ai_img"
    tmp_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    edges_path = tmp_dir / f"edges_{img_hash}.png"

    detect_edges(
        str(in_path),
        str(edges_path),
        canny_low=50,
        canny_high=150,
        blur_sigma=1.4,
    )

    advisories: list[Advisory] = []

    # 3. SKB-04 calibrator — derive mm_per_px from edge image.
    # Falls back to 1.0 if the edge image cannot be read or yields no contours.
    scale = _FALLBACK_SCALE_MM_PER_PX
    try:
        from bim_ai.skb.calibrator import calibrate_from_edges

        edges_arr = _load_edge_array(str(edges_path))
        if edges_arr is not None:
            derived = calibrate_from_edges(edges_arr)
            if derived > 0:
                scale = derived
    except Exception:
        scale = _FALLBACK_SCALE_MM_PER_PX

    # 4. Polygon recovery (Hough + contours)
    rooms, walls, openings, poly_advisories = recover_rooms(
        edges_path=str(edges_path),
        scale_mm_per_px=scale,
        image_width_px=width_px,
        image_height_px=height_px,
    )
    advisories.extend(poly_advisories)

    # 5. SKB-07 colour sampler — populate detectedTypeKey per room.
    rooms = _apply_colour_type_hints(in_path, rooms, cv_img)

    # 6. OCR (graceful fallback)
    ocr_labels, ocr_advisories = extract_labels(str(in_path), scale)
    advisories.extend(ocr_advisories)

    # Check for no_dimensions_detected advisory (OCR found no numeric labels).
    has_numeric = any(any(c.isdigit() for c in lbl.text) for lbl in ocr_labels)
    if not has_numeric and not any(a.code == "tesseract_unavailable" for a in advisories):
        advisories.append(Advisory(code="no_dimensions_detected"))

    # 7. Assemble
    layout = StructuredLayout(
        schemaVersion="img-v3.0",
        imageMetadata=ImageMetadata(
            widthPx=width_px,
            heightPx=height_px,
            calibrationMmPerPx=scale,
            briefPath=str(brief_path) if brief_path else None,
        ),
        rooms=rooms,
        walls=walls,
        openings=openings,
        ocrLabels=ocr_labels,
        advisories=_dedup_advisories(advisories),
    )
    return layout


def _load_edge_array(edges_path: str):  # type: ignore[return]
    """Load an edge image as a numpy uint8 greyscale array. Returns None on failure."""
    try:
        import cv2  # type: ignore[import-not-found]

        arr = cv2.imread(edges_path, cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)
        return arr  # may be None if file missing
    except ImportError:
        pass
    try:
        import numpy as np  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
        from PIL import Image as _Image

        arr = np.asarray(_Image.open(edges_path).convert("L"), dtype=np.uint8)
        return arr
    except Exception:
        return None


def _apply_colour_type_hints(
    image_path: Path,
    rooms: list[RoomRegion],
    cv_img: Any | None,  # type: ignore[name-defined]
) -> list[RoomRegion]:
    """Call SKB-07 colour sampler on each room polygon to populate detectedTypeKey.

    Returns a new list with updated rooms (immutable Pydantic model_copy).
    Gracefully no-ops if colour_sampler is unavailable or image cannot be loaded.
    """
    try:
        from bim_ai.skb.colour_sampler import sample
    except ImportError:
        return rooms

    # Load BGR image array once.
    img_arr = cv_img
    if img_arr is None:
        try:
            import cv2  # type: ignore[import-not-found]

            img_arr = cv2.imread(str(image_path))
        except ImportError:
            try:
                import numpy as np  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
                from PIL import Image as _Image

                pil = _Image.open(str(image_path)).convert("RGB")
                arr = np.asarray(pil, dtype=np.uint8)
                img_arr = arr[..., ::-1].copy()  # RGB → BGR
            except Exception:
                img_arr = None

    if img_arr is None:
        return rooms

    updated: list[RoomRegion] = []
    for room in rooms:
        type_key = room.detected_type_key
        if type_key is None:
            try:
                pixel_pts = [(int(pt.x), int(pt.y)) for pt in room.polygon_mm]
                if len(pixel_pts) >= 3:
                    type_key = sample(img_arr, pixel_pts)
            except Exception:
                type_key = None
        if type_key is not None and type_key != room.detected_type_key:
            room = room.model_copy(update={"detected_type_key": type_key})
        updated.append(room)
    return updated


def _file_sha256_prefix(path: Path, n: int = 16) -> str:
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with open(path, "rb") as f:
        while chunk := f.read(65536):
            h.update(chunk)
    return h.hexdigest()[:n]


def _dedup_advisories(advisories: list[Advisory]) -> list[Advisory]:
    seen: set[str] = set()
    out: list[Advisory] = []
    for a in advisories:
        if a.code not in seen:
            seen.add(a.code)
            out.append(a)
    return out

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  js:
    # Keep Playwright screenshot tests off `ubuntu-latest`, whose image churn
    # can shift flex layout/heights against committed Linux baselines.
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    timeout-minutes: 25
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # packageManager field in repo root package.json selects the pnpm version (do not duplicate here).

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: pnpm

      - run: pnpm install

      - run: pnpm verify

      - run: cd packages/web && pnpm exec playwright install chromium --with-deps

      - run: cd packages/web && pnpm exec playwright test
        env:
          CI: true

      - name: Evidence CI correlation hint (bundles reviewer mapping)
        if: always()
        env:
          CORR_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          CORR_SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
          CORR_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
          CORR_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
        run: |
          mkdir -p packages/web/test-results
          {
            echo "Map Playwright PNGs to semantic evidence fields:"
            echo "  GET /api/models/{id}/evidence-package -> suggestedEvidenceArtifactBasename,"
            echo "  semanticDigestPrefix16, suggestedEvidenceBundleFilenames.evidencePackageJson,"
            echo "  deterministicSheetEvidence[].correlation + playwrightSuggestedFilenames (png/svg/pdf/rasterPlaceholderProbe),"
            echo "  deterministicSheetEvidence[].printRasterPngHref + sheetPrintRasterIngest_v1"
            echo "    (svgContentSha256, placeholderPngSha256, diffCorrelation.playwrightBaselineSlot e.g. pngFullSheet),"
            echo "  GET /api/models/{id}/exports/sheet-print-raster.png — deterministic 128×112 print-surrogate PNG (96px viewport layout stamp + 16px titleblock band + SVG salt);"
            echo "    response headers: X-Bim-Ai-Sheet-Print-Raster-Contract, X-Bim-Ai-Sheet-Svg-Sha256, width/height."
            echo "  deterministicPlanViewEvidence[].playwrightSuggestedFilenames.pngPlanCanvas,"
            echo "  deterministicSectionCutEvidence[].playwrightSuggestedFilenames.pngSectionViewport,"
            echo "  deterministic3dViewEvidence[].playwrightSuggestedFilenames.pngViewport."
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.expectedDeterministicPngBasenames — flat CI grep target vs PNG artifact names;"
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.pixelDiffExpectation — reserved metadata when attaching programmatic diff outputs;"
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.screenshotHintGaps_v1 — deterministic rows missing required Playwright PNG filename slots;"
            echo "  evidenceLifecycleSignal_v1 — programmatic rollup (counts + digest + basename) for scripts/agents;"
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.pixelDiffExpectation.artifactIngestCorrelation_v1.ingestManifestDigestSha256 — deterministic SHA-256 over canonical ingest checklist pairs (sorted baseline/diff basenames)."
            echo "  evidenceLifecycleSignal_v1.artifactIngestManifestDigestSha256 — mirrors ingest correlation digest when pixelDiffExpectation carries artifactIngestCorrelation_v1."
            echo "  evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1.blockerCodes — grep: artifact_ingest_correlation_digest_mismatch (ingest correlation digest must match ingestChecklist_v1.targets recompute)."
            echo "  evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1 — derivative blocker rollup (correlation, screenshot slots, pixel diff ingest, artifact_ingest_correlation_digest_mismatch) + agent remediate action hook."

            echo "  evidenceBaselineLifecycleReadout_v1 — per-target baseline lifecycle rollup (committedFixtureStatus, digestCorrelationStatus, suggestedNextAction, ciGateHint); digest-excluded derivative summary."
            echo "  semanticDigestSha256 omits evidenceReviewPerformanceGate_v1 (derivative rollup; stable row digests)."
            echo "  evidenceAgentFollowThrough_v1 — stagedArtifactLinks_v1 (resolutionMode, exportRelativePaths, env-gated GitHub Actions run URLs;"
            echo "    no secrets/network), plus BCF/issue coordination checks, evidenceRef gaps, replayConflict hints (digest-excluded)."
            echo "  Python pytest scale gate: test_bundle_replay_diagnostics_large_list_under_budget (replayDiagnostics metadata scan)."
            echo "  evidenceClosureReview_v1.pixelDiffExpectation.thresholdPolicy_v1 — advisory diff thresholds for client ingest."

          name: evidence-web-${{ github.run_id }}-playwright
          retention-days: 14
          path: |
            packages/web/playwright-report

  python:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: app
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.12'

      - name: Install uv
        run: pip install uv

      - name: Verify lockfile is up to date
        run: uv lock --check

      - name: Install Python deps from lockfile
        run: uv sync --frozen --extra dev --extra ifc

      - name: Import FastAPI app (route wiring regression guard)
        run: PYTHONPATH=. uv run python -c "from bim_ai.main import app"

      - run: uv run ruff check bim_ai tests

      - run: pytest

      - name: Optional evidence-package staging probe
        env:
          BIM_AI_EVIDENCE_PROBE_BASE_URL: ${{ vars.BIM_AI_EVIDENCE_PROBE_BASE_URL }}
          BIM_AI_EVIDENCE_PROBE_MODEL_ID: ${{ vars.BIM_AI_EVIDENCE_PROBE_MODEL_ID }}
        run: bash scripts/ci-evidence-package-probe.sh

doc = Document(revision=1, elements={'internal_origin': InternalOriginElem(kind='internal_origin', id='internal_origin')}, design_option_sets=[], tool_prefs={})
cmd = CreatePropertyDefinitionCmd(type='create_property_definition', id='pd-1', key='cost_per_m2', label='Cost/m²', prop_kind='currency', enum_values=None, default_value=None, applies_to=['wall'], show_in_schedule=True)
    def apply_inplace(
        doc: Document,
        cmd: Command,
        *,
        source_provider: SourceDocProvider | None = None,
    ) -> None:
        """Apply ``cmd`` to ``doc`` in place.
    
        ``source_provider`` is optional: only ``RunClashTestCmd`` (FED-02) consults
        it to walk linked source documents for cross-link clash detection. All
        other commands ignore it. The route layer threads a real DB-backed
        provider; tests and intra-host paths can omit it.
        """
        els = doc.elements
        _enforce_linked_readonly(cmd)
        _enforce_pin_block(els, cmd)
        match cmd:
            case CreateLevelCmd():
                eid = cmd.id or new_id()
                if eid in els:
                    raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{eid}'")
                parent = cmd.parent_level_id
                if parent is not None and parent not in els:
                    raise ValueError("createLevel.parentLevelId must reference an existing Level")
                if parent is not None and not isinstance(els[parent], LevelElem):
                    raise ValueError("createLevel.parentLevelId must reference a Level")
                parent_el = els[parent] if parent is not None else None
                elev_mm = (
                    expected_level_elevation_from_parent(parent_el, cmd.offset_from_parent_mm)
                    if isinstance(parent_el, LevelElem)
                    else float(cmd.elevation_mm)
                )
                els[eid] = LevelElem(
                    kind="level",
                    id=eid,
                    name=cmd.name,
                    elevation_mm=elev_mm,
                    datum_kind=cmd.datum_kind,
                    parent_level_id=parent,
                    offset_from_parent_mm=cmd.offset_from_parent_mm,
                )
                propagate_dependent_level_elevations(els)
                # VIE-05: optionally create a companion "<name> — Plan" plan view in
                # the same step so the common path (level then plan) is one action.
                if cmd.also_create_plan_view:
                    pv_id = cmd.plan_view_id or new_id()
                    if pv_id in els:
                        raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{pv_id}'")
                    els[pv_id] = PlanViewElem(
                        kind="plan_view",
                        id=pv_id,
                        name=f"{cmd.name} — Plan",
                        level_id=eid,
                    )
    
            case CreateWallCmd():
                eid = cmd.id or new_id()
                if eid in els:
                    raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{eid}'")
                if cmd.level_id not in els or not isinstance(els[cmd.level_id], LevelElem):
                    raise ValueError("createWall.levelId must reference an existing Level")
                # TOP-V3-04: validate site host toposolid exists when specified.
                if cmd.site_host_id is not None:

OK - `LevelElem` has `elevation_mm` as the field name and `elevationMm` as alias. With `populate_by_name=True`, `elevation_mm=0.0` works in the constructor. ✓

Let me summarize all changes made:

**Files modified:**

1. `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/pyproject.toml` - added `bcrypt>=4.0,<5` and `scikit-image>=0.22,<1` to main dependencies

2. `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/uv.lock` - updated by running `uv lock` (adds bcrypt 4.3.0 and scikit-image 0.26.0)

3. `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/api/registry.py` - added `draw-detail-region` and `update-material-pbr` tool descriptors

4. `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py`:
   - Added `CreateScheduleViewCmd` and `SetElementPropCmd` to imports
   - Fixed `CreatePropertyDefinitionCmd` handler (was using wrong fields from a schedule view)
   - Added `SetElementPropCmd` case handler
   - Added `CreateScheduleViewCmd` case handler

5. `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/constraints.py`:
   - Added `ToposolidElem` import
   - Added `"toposolid_pierce_check": "site"` to `_RULE_DISCIPLINE`
   - Added `"toposolid_pierce_check": "documentation"` to `_RULE_BLOCKING_CLASS`
   - Added `_toposolid_pierce_check_violations()` function
   - Called it from `evaluate()`

6. `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/test_create_link_dxf_command.py` - changed `pytest.raises(ValueError)` to check `ok is False` and match in `code`

7. `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/test_vie_03_elevation_view.py` - same fix, removed unused `pytest` import

8. `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/test_vie_07_pin_element.py` - same fix for all 3 failing tests, removed unused `pytest` import

Here is a summary of all the fixes implemented:

---

**Group 1 - Missing dependencies:**

- Added `bcrypt>=4.0,<5` and `scikit-image>=0.22,<1` to `app/pyproject.toml` main dependencies
- Ran `uv lock` from the `app/` directory to update the lockfile (adds bcrypt 4.3.0, scikit-image 0.26.0, and transitive deps: imageio, lazy-loader, networkx, scipy, tifffile)

**Group 2 - Missing tool descriptors:**

- Added `draw-detail-region` descriptor (category=mutation) to `bim_ai/api/registry.py`
- Added `update-material-pbr` descriptor (category=mutation) to `bim_ai/api/registry.py`

**Group 3 - Missing engine handlers:**

- Added `CreateScheduleViewCmd` and `SetElementPropCmd` to the import block in `bim_ai/engine.py`
- Fixed `CreatePropertyDefinitionCmd` handler — it was incorrectly using schedule-view fields (`name`, `category`, `columns`, etc.) instead of the property-definition fields (`key`, `label`, `propKind`, `appliesTo`, `showInSchedule`)
- Added `SetElementPropCmd` case handler: resolves the target element by `elementId`, raises `ValueError` if not found, merges the new `key/value` into the element's `props` dict
- Added `CreateScheduleViewCmd` case handler: creates a `ScheduleElem` with `category`, `columns`, `filterExpr`, `sortKey`, `sortDir`

**Group 4 - pytest.raises(ValueError) on try_commit_bundle:**

`try_commit_bundle` catches `ValueError` internally and returns `(False, None, cmds, [], str(exc))` — it never re-raises. The tests were wrongly using `pytest.raises`. Fixed in three test files by switching to the `ok, new_doc, _, _, code = try_commit_bundle(...)` pattern and asserting `ok is False` + `"error phrase" in code`:

- `tests/test_create_link_dxf_command.py::test_create_link_dxf_rejects_unknown_level`
- `tests/test_vie_03_elevation_view.py::test_custom_direction_requires_angle`
- `tests/test_vie_07_pin_element.py` — three tests: `test_move_wall_endpoints_refuses_pinned_wall_without_override`, `test_pinned_grid_blocks_move_grid_endpoints`, `test_unpin_unknown_element_raises`

**Group 5 - Logic failures:**

- Implemented `_toposolid_pierce_check_violations()` in `bim_ai/constraints.py`: warns (non-blocking) when a `FloorElem` boundary overlaps a `ToposolidElem` boundary; suppressed when any `SlabOpeningElem` hosted on that floor exists; called from `evaluate()`. Added `"toposolid_pierce_check": "site"` to `_RULE_DISCIPLINE` and `"toposolid_pierce_check": "documentation"` to `_RULE_BLOCKING_CLASS`
- `test_ifc_shadow_import` and `test_advisor_blocking_class_expansion` not fixed per instructions (the first test in advisor expansion still fails because `monitored_source_drift` is missing from `_RULE_BLOCKING_CLASS`, a pre-existing bug unrelated to the ruff commit)
